On June 25th, 2024, an Otero County deputy responds to reports of a man walking alongside a dark highway in Mescalero, New Mexico. What began as a welfare check rapidly spirals into an intense barrage of gunfire, sending shock waves through the community. However, the investigation takes a shocking turn when they review the officer's dash cam footage, exposing a disturbing truth they never saw coming.

The following report is based on official police records, and most of the footage has never been seen before. Please note that all persons charged with a crime are presumed innocent unless and until proven guilty in a court of law.
It's just after 10:00 p.m. when 911 dispatchers receive a troubling call from a concerned driver on Highway 70 near the Mescalero reservation.
There is a pedestrian walking in the median by our Lady of Guadalupe Church, and I thought it was an elk and I almost hit him and I think they're intoxicated.

27-year-old Deputy Jacob Diaz Austin responds to the call. He arrives to find a young male walking alongside the highway, who would later be identified as 17-year-old Elijah Hadley. An autopsy report later revealed that 17-year-old Elijah Hadley suffered a total of 13 gunshot wounds. It's been widely reported by different news outlets that Deputy Jacob Diaz Austin was placed on paid administrative leave until he was reinstated to active duty nearly three months later on September 16th, 2024, though we haven't been able to verify this. Elijah would later be identified as a member of the nearby Mescalero Apache tribe. A tribal representative attempted to verify news of the deputy's reinstatement, but the district attorney assigned to the case declined to comment. On January 13th, 2025, six months after Elijah's tragic death, Jacob was officially charged with first-degree murder. On January 27th, Jacob's legal team filed a motion to dismiss the case, but the state of New Mexico ruled that Jacob acted with a deliberate intention to take Elijah's life, upholding the charges. A motion to seal the conditions of his release was approved the following day, citing concerns for the safety of Jacob and his immediate family. The case is ongoing, with a preliminary hearing scheduled for February 26th, 2025.
